Rice simmered in hojicha (roasted green tea) + water — naturally gluten-free and vegan. This porridge is the reliable GF core of every set here; eat it plain or with GF pickles.

Seasonal 12-course kaiseki around the cha-gayu: sashimi, grilled ayu, simmered veg/fish, etc. Bring your own tamari for sashimi (the soy tare is wheat). Confirm dashi/soy in nimono; ask to omit 生麩 nama-fu (wheat gluten) and any 蒲焼き kabayaki (soy/wheat sauce). The porridge and plain grilled/salted fish are the safe parts. Contains fish — not vegan.

items.en: Cha-gayu bento (lacquer box of 12-13 small dishes + porridge + soup)
items.note: Fixed box confirmed to contain wheat items: 生麩 nama-fu (wheat gluten), さんま蒲焼き sanma kabayaki (soy/wheat sauce), and soy-simmered dishes (高野豆腐 etc.). Not GF as served. The cha-gayu porridge and sesame tofu are GF-adjacent — ask which components can be swapped or removed.
Smaller kaiseki built on the cha-gayu. Same cautions as the full kaiseki: own tamari for sashimi, confirm dashi/soy, omit nama-fu (wheat gluten) and kabayaki. Contains fish.

items.en: Sesame tofu with pickles and dessert
items.note: House sesame tofu is traditionally bound with 葛 kuzu (kudzu) starch, which is gluten-free — confirm no wheat starch is added. Comes with pickles (ask: nara-zuke uses sake lees) and dessert (confirm dessert is GF).

items.ja: わらび餅おうすセット
items.note: Matcha (おうす) is GF. Warabimochi can be pure warabi/kuzu starch (GF) but many versions blend in wheat starch — confirm it is bracken/kuzu starch only, no wheat, and that any kinako dusting is uncontaminated. Ask about honey/dairy for vegan.

items.en: Daily special lunch (main, rice, soup — refillable)
items.note: All-vegan set of rotating mains + rice + soup. GF risk: regular shoyu (wheat) and soy/wheat-gluten mock meats. Ask which mains are wheat-free, request tamari/salt seasoning, and confirm no seitan. Rice is the safe base.

items.ja: 日替わりランチセット
items.en: Daily lunch set (with coffee/tea, fruit, dessert)
items.note: Same as daily lunch plus drink/fruit/dessert. Ask about shoyu (wheat), mock-meat gluten, and whether the dessert is wheat-free.

items.ja: ランチメニュー（4種の主菜）
items.en: Lunch menu — 4 rotating mains, soup, rice, drink, dessert
items.note: Choice of 4 vegan mains. Vegan throughout; GF depends on the day. Ask for salt/tamari-seasoned dishes, avoid soy-sauce-braised and mock-meat (wheat-gluten) items, keep to rice + veg.
Vegan, but Japanese-style curry roux is thickened with wheat flour — not GF unless they confirm a wheat-free roux. Ask.
Vegan, but spring-roll wrappers are wheat flour — not GF.

items.note: Black drip coffee is GF. For vegan ask about plant milk vs dairy.

items.en: Bolik soup set meal (rotating: one washoku, one international/European soup)
items.note: Soup-centred set that changes every 1-2 months. GF and vegan both depend on the current soup — confirm no wheat/roux, that the soy seasoning is tamari, no bread on the side, and vegetable (not meat) stock.
Russian-style set incl. drink. Borscht base is usually GF (beets/veg) but confirm no wheat thickening and skip the bread; Russian dishes often use meat stock + sour cream, so ask.
Beet soup is naturally GF and can be vegan, but Russian borscht commonly has meat stock and smetana (sour cream) — confirm vegetable stock, no wheat, no cream for vegan.
Wheat-flour baked goods — not GF. Confirm dairy/egg for vegan.

nara_shika_no_fune_kamado.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Set meals rotate seasonally; rice sells out by ~15:00.

items.note: Freshly cooked kamado white rice — naturally GF and vegan; the safe core of any set here. Ask whether you can order rice + oboro tofu + pickles without the fried/tempura main.

items.en: Fried horse-mackerel set (aji fry, oboro tofu, small dish, rice, miso soup, pickles)
items.note: Aji fry is breaded/deep-fried (panko = wheat). Also, this set's miso soup is made with 春日はくたくうどん (Kasuga haku-taku udon = wheat noodles), so even the soup contains wheat. Not GF as served — ask to swap out the fried main and the udon-in-soup; the kamado rice + oboro tofu are the GF parts.

items.en: Seasonal tempura set (5 tempura, oboro tofu, small dish, rice, miso soup, pickles)
items.note: Tempura uses wheat-flour batter, and the set's miso soup contains 春日はくたくうどん (wheat udon). Not GF. The kamado rice + oboro tofu + pickles are the GF-adjacent items — ask to build a plate from those.
Soft curd tofu is GF if eaten with salt; ask about the tare/topping (regular soy sauce = wheat). Request salt 塩 instead. Confirm no bonito-dashi topping for vegan.

Made from 100% Yoshino honkuzu (arrowroot starch) — naturally gluten-free, no wheat. Served with kuromitsu (black-sugar syrup) + kinako (roasted soy flour), both gf/vegan. This is a kuzu manufacturer (Inoue Tengyokudo), so the kuzu is authentic and pure.
Kuzu-mochi (pure kuzu, gf) + matcha (gf/vegan).
Kuzu noodle strands dipped in kuromitsu. Pure kuzu starch, no wheat — gf/vegan.
Kuzu-kiri + matcha.
Kuzu-kiri with sweetened adzuki (azuki beans + sugar) — gf/vegan.

items.ja: 葛まんじゅう（抹茶セット）
items.note: Kuzu-manju = anko wrapped in translucent kuzu dough (not wheat manju). Confirm on-site the wrapper is kuzu only (it is at this specialist). gf/vegan.

items.ja: 葛ぷりんセット（ドリンク付）
items.note: Kuzu-thickened pudding — no wheat, gf. Contains dairy (and possibly egg) so NOT vegan.

items.ja: Kudzuデザートセット（ドリンク付）
items.note: Assorted kuzu dessert plate — kuzu base is gf, but confirm no wheat accompaniment (cookie/wafer) and no dairy before ordering.
Made from sesame + kuzu (no soybean, no wheat) — the tofu itself is gf/vegan. Ask about the dip: request salt 塩 or wasabi only; skip any soy tare (regular soy sauce = wheat).
Flavoured kuzu-kiri — kuzu base gf. Confirm the seasonal flavouring/syrup (a milk or matcha-milk version would not be vegan).
Rice porridge thickened with kuzu (both gf). Ask about the broth/seasoning — dashi may contain wheat soy sauce and/or bonito (not vegan). Request a salt-only / wheat-free preparation.
Udon = WHEAT noodle. Even 'kuzu udon' here is blended with wheat flour. Not gluten-free — avoid.
Wheat-flour udon (kuzu-blended) — contains wheat. Avoid.
Wheat-flour udon — contains wheat. Avoid.
Multi-dish set — likely includes udon and various seasoned small dishes. Vet each component; regular soy sauce/dashi = wheat. Ask staff to build a kuzu-and-rice, wheat-free version.
100% Yoshino honkuzu, handmade to order (~10 min). Dipped in kuromitsu (black-sugar syrup). Pure kuzu starch, no wheat — gf/vegan.
100% Yoshino kuzu, made to order. gf/vegan (served with kinako/kuromitsu).
Kuzu-mochi with sweetened adzuki — gf/vegan.
Sweet adzuki-bean soup with kuzu-mochi. Adzuki + sugar + kuzu — gf/vegan. Price not published; confirm on-site.
Adzuki soup with kuzu — gf/vegan. Confirm price on-site.

Shaved ice topped with kuzu-mochi + syrup (choose kuromitsu, matcha, brown-sugar milk, or matcha-milk). Ice + syrup + kuzu are gf. Plain syrups are vegan; milk-based syrups are NOT vegan — pick kuromitsu/matcha for vegan.
Bracken-starch (warabiko) jelly with kinako + kuromitsu — starch-based, no wheat, gf/vegan. Confirm it is warabi/potato starch, not a wheat-extended version.
Whisked matcha — gf/vegan.
Roasted green tea — gf/vegan.

CELIAC WARNING: this shop also serves 生麩田楽 (nama-fu dengaku) — nama-fu is raw WHEAT GLUTEN and is NOT gf. Confirm dengaku is 豆腐田楽 (tofu), not 麩 (fu). Even tofu dengaku's miso topping may contain barley/wheat and dengaku-miso sometimes has egg — ask.

Tofu simmered in soy-milk broth — the tofu is gf/vegan. The dipping tare/ponzu contains wheat soy sauce: request salt 塩 or bring your own tamari. This is a course with ~12 tofu side dishes — vet each; several are simmered in wheat soy/dashi.
Six tofu preparations (yudofu, oboro-dofu, nama-atsuage, okara, hirousu, dengaku). Mixed gf risk — see individual items. Ask to swap out okara/fu items and use salt.

Best ask-tier pick: soft tofu served with a CHOICE of 塩 (salt) / だし醤油 (dashi-soy) / 柚子胡椒 (yuzu pepper). Choose 塩 SALT → gf/vegan. Avoid the dashi-shoyu option (wheat). Yuzu-kosho usually gf but confirm.
Deep-fried tofu with no flour coating (tofu only). Ask that the fryer oil is not shared with tempura/breaded items, and request salt instead of soy dip.
Deep-fried tofu, no batter. Confirm clean/dedicated fryer oil; dip may be wheat soy — ask for salt.
Okara (unohana) is almost always simmered with soy sauce (wheat) and dashi — treat as containing wheat. Also dashi is usually bonito (not vegan). Avoid unless confirmed tamari-based.
Tofu-and-vegetable fritter (ganmodoki). The fritter is usually flour-free but is served/simmered in a dashi-soy broth (wheat) — confirm; may also contain bonito dashi.
Soy-milk broth with tofu/veg — soy milk and tofu are gf. Confirm the broth seasoning is wheat-free (no soy sauce/wheat dashi) and no bonito for vegan.
Confirm 豆腐 (tofu) not 生麩/wheat-gluten dengaku. Miso topping may contain barley/wheat — ask.
Multi-course tofu meal. Vet each dish; regular soy sauce/dashi = wheat. Ask staff to prepare salt-seasoned, wheat-free versions and to omit any 麩 (wheat gluten).
Duck hotpot (not vegan). Broth typically contains wheat soy sauce — confirm a tamari/wheat-free broth. Reserve 2 days ahead.

nara_houseki_bako.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Menu ROTATES daily/seasonally (300+ flavours, almost no fixed menu) — the specific flavours below are examples from past seasons and may not be available; verify on-site.

items.ja: エスプーマ（ミルク／ヨーグルト）
items.note: Core to this shop: kakigori topped with milk-espuma or yogurt-espuma foam. Espuma is dairy/cream + N2O — no wheat itself (gf), but NOT vegan. The gf risk is solid toppings — see flavour notes.

Shaved ice + strawberry syrup + milk espuma. Ice+syrup base is gf; espuma is dairy (not vegan). ASK whether any wheat topping (cookie crumble, castella/sponge, cereal) is added — many of this shop's creations include such garnishes.
Fruit syrup + yogurt espuma. Base gf; dairy (not vegan). Ask about solid toppings (wheat crumble/sponge).
Kinako (roasted soy flour) is gf. Ice+syrup base gf. Ask about espuma (dairy → not vegan) and any wheat garnish.
Colour-changing ice (butterfly-pea + citrus). Ice+syrup base is gf/vegan; ASK about espuma (dairy) and any wheat topping before treating as safe.
Japanese curry — assume WHEAT-flour roux. Not verified gluten-free. Contains chicken. Avoid unless the shop explicitly confirms a flour-free curry.

items.ja: 大和牛マイルドカレー
items.note: Japanese curry roux = wheat. Not gf. Contains beef.
Wheat-flour roux. Not gf.
Wheat-flour roux. Not gf.
Wheat-flour roux. Not gf.

items.ja: スペシャルあいがけカレー
items.note: Wheat-flour roux. Not gf.
Baked curry with wheat roux (and likely cheese). Not gf.
Wheat-flour roux + egg. Not gf.
Ramen noodles = WHEAT, plus wheat curry. Definitely not gf.
Vegetarian, but Japanese curry roux still contains WHEAT flour → not gf. Vegan status unconfirmed (roux/dairy) — ask.

items.ja: 自然栽培 大和茶抹茶 お抹茶セット
items.note: Matcha itself is GF/vegan; the accompanying wagashi varies — ask if the sweet is rice/bean-based (no wheat) and egg-free.
Shaved ice is GF; ask that syrup/anko topping contains no barley (mugi) and, for vegan, no condensed milk.

items.ja: 発酵あんこ 米糀甘酒ぜんざい
items.note: Red bean + rice-koji amazake + mochi are naturally GF/plant-based; confirm mochi is 100% rice and amazake is rice-only (no barley/mugi koji).
Rice-based set; ask which sides use soy sauce/miso and request salt-seasoned or plain versions, or bring own tamari.

Juwari (100% buckwheat) noodle is GF — but confirm the noodle is truly 10-wari with NO wheat flour and no shared boiling water with wheat udon. The tsuyu dipping sauce contains soy sauce (wheat) + dashi (fish): bring your own tamari to dip instead.
Same as mori soba — confirm 100% buckwheat / no shared water; dip with own tamari instead of the soy-based tsuyu.
Served in a hot broth made with soy sauce (wheat) — not fixable by request unless they will serve the plain drained noodle with your own tamari.
Noodle served in its hot cooking water, dipped in tsuyu; confirm 100% buckwheat / dedicated water and dip with own tamari (tsuyu contains soy sauce).

Same as above; sold by the piece (¥130 each). Confirm no wheat-flour dusting.
Boxed set; same rice/mugwort/red-bean recipe. Confirm no wheat-flour dusting.

Among pickles avoid soy-sauce ones (醤油漬); narazuke is sake-lees (alcoholic, no wheat); monaka wafer = wheat (skip). Stick to plain rice + chagayu + vinegar/miso/koji/salt pickles; confirm each. nara_nihon_ryori_tekisui.price_note: Set kaiseki only — no a-la-carte. Lunch gozen approx ¥3,800/¥5,500, dinner kaiseki from ¥10,000, chef's course ~¥15,000 (+tax/service). Dishes are seasonal and change; components below are the fixed course structure from the restaurant's own site. Celiac MUST notify at reservation (0742-81-8236) — a kaiseki kitchen can adapt. Prices approximate — may change.

items.note: Caffeine-free sweet herbal tea; naturally gluten-free and vegan.
Content varies daily; often dressed in soy/dashi. Ask kitchen to prep without wheat soy and specify vegan.
Mixed bites, some likely soy-marinated or with fish. Ask for wheat-free / vegan substitutions dish by dish.
Bonito/kombu dashi (non-vegan) usually seasoned with usukuchi soy (wheat). Ask if seasoned with salt only / wheat-free.
Raw fish itself is GF; the soy sauce served contains wheat. Bring/use own tamari or ask for tamari for dipping.
Broth may contain soy/miso and dashi. Ask for a salt or wheat-free-tamari base and vegetable/vegan version.
Ask for shio-yaki (salt-grilled, 塩) instead of soy/tare glaze to keep it GF.

items.ja: 食事 / 自然薯とろろ

Fresh seasonal fruit; naturally GF and vegan (confirm no wheat-based sweet accompaniment).

nara_com_ngon.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Vietnamese food is largely rice-based, so many dishes are naturally close to GF; the main risk is soy sauce / hoisin dipping sauces and shared fryer oil.

items.en: Fresh spring rolls (rice paper, shrimp, herbs)
items.note: Rice-paper roll itself is GF; contains shrimp. Dipping sauce is the risk — use fish-sauce nuoc cham or own tamari, NOT hoisin (wheat). Ask.
Vietnamese cha gio use rice-paper wrappers (GF) but confirm no wheat wrapper and a non-shared fryer; served with lettuce/herbs.

items.ja: 春巻きの前菜 天秤盛り
items.note: Combo of fresh and fried rolls; same caveats — dipping sauce and fryer. Contains shrimp.

Rice noodles GF; confirm broth seasoning is wheat-free. Contains beef.
Rice vermicelli GF; spicy broth may contain shrimp paste and soy. Ask about wheat in the seasoning.

items.en: Rice-noodle soup with pork, shrimp, quail egg
items.note: Flat rice noodles GF; broth seasoning is the risk. Ask wheat-free.

items.en: Saigon mixed rice (ground meat, cheese, fried egg)

items.note: Fried chicken is typically wheat-floured/battered — avoid unless kitchen confirms rice-flour coating. Treat as contains wheat.

items.ja: チキンココナッツカレー

nara_rahotsu.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Kakigori (shaved ice) is year-round and the main draw; Thai food/lunch is seasonal (roughly Oct–Apr) and weekly-changing. Reservations via AirReserve only. Menu rotates fast (some kakigori last 1–2 weeks).

items.ja: カオニャオ・マムアン（マンゴースティッキーライス）

Shaved ice + mango/coconut syrup + fresh mango is likely GF; yogurt cream topping is dairy. Ask to confirm syrups wheat-free and to omit dairy cream for vegan.
Coconut milk syrup, Mimolette cheese, black pepper, salt, crispy fried onions. Crispy fried onions are often wheat-flour dusted — ask to omit. Contains dairy (cheese).
Jasmine/mango/almond syrups over almond tofu. Ask syrups are wheat-free; almond tofu may use dairy/gelatin — ask for vegan.

Rice noodles are GF; risk is soy sauce in the wok. Tamarind/fish-sauce based versions are GF — ask for no soy sauce. Contains egg/shrimp/fish sauce.

items.en: Green curry with Yamato chicken & coconut milk

Base (lemongrass, lime, chili, fish sauce) is usually GF. CAUTION: a noodle version uses Miwa somen (三輪素麺 = WHEAT) — order plain/rice, not the somen version. Ask about soy sauce.
Marinade may contain soy sauce; peanut dipping sauce sometimes has soy/wheat. Ask for soy-free marinade and check the sauce.

items.note: Notify a gluten allergy 3 business days ahead. Ask for sashimi with your own tamari, grilled fish and Yamato beef seasoned with salt not soy-based tare, and plain rice.

items.en: Celebration kaiseki (with whole grilled sea bream)
items.note: The whole sea bream can be salt-grilled (塩焼き) — ask for salt not tare. Notify gluten allergy 3 business days ahead.
Fresh sashimi — gluten-free if you use your own tamari instead of the house soy sauce.
Ask for the fish grilled with salt (塩焼き) rather than a soy/mirin tare.

items.ja: 米粉アボカドチキンサンド
items.note: Rice-flour bun, but this is a wheat bakery — 米粉パン commonly has added wheat gluten to rise, and there is no allergen separation. Not celiac-safe unless staff confirm wheat-free AND separate handling.
Same shared-wheat-kitchen cross-contamination risk as other 米粉 items.
Contains dairy; same wheat cross-contamination risk.
Wheat dough plus wheat curry roux plus fried breading. Not fixable.

items.ja: あまおう苺とクランベリーのベーグル
items.note: Made with 国産小麦 (domestic wheat) — explicitly wheat.
Wheat pastry.

items.ja: 薬膳やさいスープカレー
items.section: 薬膳野菜＋石焼スープカレー
items.note: Soup curry (スープカレー) is a thin spice broth, NOT wheat-thickened roux, so it can be made GF. Ask staff to confirm the broth/seasoning has no 小麦 (wheat) or 醤油 (soy sauce). Broth base is chicken + niboshi dashi so not vegan. Served with plain rice (GF).

items.ja: 薬膳チキンスープカレー
items.section: 薬膳野菜＋石焼スープカレー
items.note: Flourless spice broth. Ask to confirm no wheat/soy sauce in the seasoning; pair with plain rice for a GF meal.

items.ja: 薬膳角煮スープカレー
items.section: 薬膳野菜＋石焼スープカレー
items.note: Higher risk: 角煮 braised pork is normally simmered in soy sauce (wheat). Prefer the vegetable or chicken version unless staff confirm a wheat-free braise.

items.ja: スープ選択：ココナッツ
items.note: Coconut base is the closest to vegan/GF; ask whether it still contains chicken/fish/soy. Original and shrimp (エビ) bases also available.
Plain steamed rice — naturally gluten-free and safe.

items.ja: 自家製キウイソースヨーグルト
items.note: Dairy dessert, gluten-free (¥250 on weekends/holidays).
茶がゆ (rice porridge cooked in hojicha tea) is rice-based and the single most likely naturally-GF item here. Ask staff to confirm the sides (pickles/tsukemono, any sauces) are free of soy sauce/wheat.

Classic French prix-fixe (no a la carte). Bread service, flour-thickened sauces and floured/breaded proteins are likely; request a GF build (no bread, flourless sauces, fish/meat grilled with salt) with advance notice. State セリアック病 / グルテンフリー when booking.
Highest lunch course — most kitchen flexibility to build a GF menu with advance reservation.
Flagship tasting course; a hotel dining room of this class can prepare a gluten-free menu with advance notice. Call ahead and state celiac/gluten-free.
Includes bread/toast — wheat. Choose the 茶がゆ or 和定食 instead.

items.ja: プランツォ・デル・ジョルノ1
items.note: Chef's fixed multi-course menu only, no a la carte. Includes wheat pasta courses plus bread, so a celiac must pre-arrange substitutions — own site asks diners to notify dietary restrictions in advance. Not walk-in GF friendly.
Grilled beef is often GF; confirm seasoning/sauce has no soy sauce or flour. A dish the kitchen could serve GF as a pasta substitute if arranged.
Fish poached in tomato/broth is often naturally GF; confirm no flour and no wheat-based stock. Good GF-substitute candidate.

items.section: アンティパスト (antipasto, within course)
items.note: Steamed octopus is likely GF; confirm no wheat in the cured soppressata or dressing.

items.ja: 生ハムとマスカルポーネのトルテッローニ
items.note: Filled wheat pasta — not fixable; must be substituted out of the course.

items.ja: カレー1種＋本日のおかず
items.note: Rice-based South Indian meals, NO naan — the celiac advantage. But ask about ヒング/アサフォエティダ (asafoetida/hing), which is usually cut with wheat flour and used in sambar/rasam/dals, and whether any fried sides (揚げ物) are wheat-battered. Vegan/veg meal possible.
Sample plate of the day's curries over rice. Same hing/fried-sides caveat — confirm wheat-free.

items.ja: ブリのココナッツカレー
items.note: Coconut-milk based curries are often flourless; confirm no wheat (hing/thickener) in the spice mix.

items.ja: 野菜のカレー（サンバル）
items.note: Lentil-and-vegetable, potentially vegan; principal hing risk is here — ask if the sambar/rasam use wheat-cut asafoetida.

items.ja: バスマティライス / ジャスミン米
items.note: Plain rice base — naturally gluten-free and safe.
Black coffee — GF and vegan.
Wheat-flour cake — not GF.
Rice-based main (ご飯もの) makes this the better bet. Ask to skip the wheat dessert and confirm the rice dish uses no curry roux or soy sauce. Set A includes bread + dessert (wheat) — avoid.

items.ja: スモークサーモンサラダ
items.note: Possibly GF if no croutons; confirm the dressing has no soy sauce/wheat.

items.ja: そば粉のピザ（マルゲリータ）
items.note: Buckwheat-flour pizza dough is typically a wheat blend. Only GF if staff confirm 十割 (100% buckwheat, no wheat) — otherwise contains wheat.

items.ja: オープンサンドランチ
items.note: 発芽玄米パン (sprouted brown-rice bread) still contains wheat — not GF.

items.ja: 特製フレンチトースト
items.note: Wheat bread — not GF.

items.ja: みむろ杉 ディオアビータ (45ml)
items.note: Pure rice junmai (rice/water/koji) — naturally gluten-free and vegan.
Junmai ginjo — rice-only, gluten-free.

items.ja: 3種飲み比べ 利き酒セット
items.note: 45ml x3. All-sake flights are gluten-free. Safe way to sample.
Barley beer — contains gluten. Avoid.

items.ja: 3種食べ比べセット（燻製豆腐・ジャーキー等）
items.en: 3-item otsumami tasting set (smoked tofu, game jerky/ham, ayu)
items.note: Treat as gluten-risk: Japanese smoking/curing and jerky marinades usually contain soy sauce (wheat). Ask staff to confirm each item is soy-sauce/wheat-free before eating.
Black self-roasted coffee — naturally gluten-free and vegan. Price not published; verify in person.

items.ja: チーズケーキとコーヒー
items.note: The coffee is GF, but the cheesecake almost certainly contains wheat flour (and dairy/egg). Order coffee alone unless the shop confirms a flourless cheesecake. Full menu is largely undocumented online — verify sweets on-site.
120+ Nara local sakes, all pure-rice — every tasting pour is naturally gluten-free and vegan. This is the strongest GF stop of the batch.

items.ja: 純米大吟醸 飲み比べ 2種
items.note: Junmai daiginjo = rice/water/koji — gluten-free.
Rotating featured pure-rice junmai — gluten-free.
Rice-koji amazake is GF, but sake-lees (酒粕) amazake is not — ask which type before drinking.
Fermented soy-and-wheat seasoning — contains wheat. Avoid.

nara_aonocafe.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Individual onigiri prices are not published; only lunch-set prices are documented. One local source lists the shop as 休業中 (on hiatus) — confirm it is open before visiting.

items.en: Ao Onigiri Lunch: 3 mini onigiri from classic fillings (mentaiko / takana / ume) + pork soup + dashimaki egg + side + pickles
items.note: Set includes pork soup (miso/dashi may contain soy/wheat) and dashimaki egg (dashi may contain soy/wheat). Of the fillings, ume is usually GF; mentaiko contains wheat; takana-zuke may be soy-seasoned. Ask to build from ume + plain salt only and whether soup/dashi are wheat-free.

items.en: Shiro Onigiri Lunch: choose 3 onigiri from ~15 original fillings + pork soup + pickles
items.note: Many of the ~15 fillings contain wheat/soy (roast-beef sauce, konbu tsukudani, mentaiko). Set also includes pork soup. Ask which fillings use no soy sauce (or tamari), and request plain salt / ume.
Umeboshi itself is usually GF, but okaka (bonito) is commonly soy-seasoned = wheat, and contains fish. Ask for a plain 梅 (umeboshi-only) onigiri.
Takana-zuke is plant-based but frequently soy-seasoned = wheat. Ask about the seasoning; request plain/tamari if celiac.

items.en: Seared cheese and spicy cod roe (mentaiko)
items.note: Mentaiko marinade typically contains wheat/soy; also contains dairy. Not GF.

items.ja: 自家製ローストビーフ
items.note: Roast-beef sauce almost always contains soy sauce = wheat. Not GF.
Konbu tsukudani is simmered in soy sauce = wheat unless tamari (unlikely). Treat as containing gluten.

nara_nara_kitamachi_shokudo_i.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Prices are from the takeout menu; dine-in may differ. Shop rotates 100+ items plus blackboard specials.

items.ja: プロシュート・ディ・パルマとモッツァレラ・ルッコラのサラダ
items.romaji: Puroshuuto di Paruma to Mottsarera rukkora no sarada
items.en: Prosciutto di Parma, mozzarella & arugula salad
items.note: Naturally wheat-free (cured ham, fresh mozzarella, rocket). Ask for no croutons/crostini and check the dressing (say グルテンアレルギー / gluten allergy). Contains pork and dairy.

items.ja: 蛍烏賊と春キャベツのアンチョビソテー
items.romaji: Hotaru-ika to haru-kyabetsu no anchobi sotee
items.en: Firefly squid & spring cabbage anchovy sauté
items.note: Seasonal. Can be wheat-free — ask for no flour and no soy sauce (醤油なし), cooked with just olive oil/garlic. Contains seafood.
Shrimp in garlic olive oil is naturally GF, but served with baguette for dipping — decline the bread (パンなしで) and confirm no flour added. Contains shellfish.

items.ja: トリッパのシチリア風煮込み
items.note: Tomato-braised beef tripe is often GF, but some kitchens flour-dust or thicken. Ask: no flour dusting, no bread/broth-cube. Contains offal.

items.ja: 三元豚スペアリブのカチャトーラ
items.note: Signature dish. Cacciatora ribs are frequently flour-dusted before browning. Ask for no flour on the meat (小麦粉なし) and no bread/roux thickener; best case, plain braised in tomato & olive oil. Contains pork.

items.ja: さだやんのシンプル炒飯
items.note: Fried rice can be GF but Japanese soy sauce/seasoning usually contains wheat. Ask for tamari or salt-only seasoning. Typically contains egg.

items.ja: ふわとろ卵のオムライス
items.note: Ketchup rice + egg is often GF, but many kitchens top omurice with wheat-based demi-glace. Ask for plain ketchup/tomato sauce, no demi-glace, no flour. Contains egg.

items.ja: ファミリア特製ナポリタンスパゲッティ
items.note: Wheat pasta — avoid. No GF pasta option indicated.

items.ja: カリカリチキンドリア
items.note: Doria = rice baked in béchamel (wheat flour) — avoid.

items.ja: 大和ポークのハヤシライス
items.note: Hayashi/demi-glace sauce is thickened with wheat flour — avoid.

items.ja: さだやんの気まぐれカレー
items.note: Japanese curry roux is wheat-flour based — avoid unless they confirm a flourless curry (rare).

nara_sahha.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Prices vary noticeably across sources (menu has changed over time); verify on arrival. Halal restaurant (no pork/alcohol).

items.ja: クスクスィアルーシュ
items.note: Couscous is wheat semolina — NOT gluten-free. The grain is the core of the dish; avoid.
Couscous = wheat semolina — NOT gluten-free.

items.en: Kabsa (Arab spiced rice, biryani-like), chicken or lamb
items.note: Spiced long-grain rice — naturally GF, but confirm no wheat in the spice mix/stock and that the meat is not floured. The owner speaks fluent Japanese; ask to confirm GF.

items.en: Shakshuka (eggs poached in spiced tomato/pepper sauce)
items.note: The stew (tomato, peppers, egg, spices) is naturally GF, but one source says it comes with naan — ask to omit the bread and confirm no flour thickener. Contains egg.
Chickpea-based, traditionally GF, but some recipes add wheat flour as binder — confirm. Order the plate, not the wrap (wrap uses bread = wheat). Plate version likely vegan.
Phyllo/pastry dough = wheat — NOT gluten-free.
Roasted coffee with cardamom, star anise, orange peel; served black. Naturally GF and vegan.

nara_kaya_cafe_naramachi_ten.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. One source flags this location as possibly closed (閉店) or weekends-only — verify operating status before visiting.

items.ja: イチゴ薫る豆腐ティラミス（ノーマル）
items.note: Signature dish. Despite being tofu-based, tiramisu is traditionally layered over wheat sponge/ladyfingers and contains mascarpone (dairy). GF NOT confirmed — treat as wheat and verify the base in person.

items.ja: 豆腐ティラミス（抹茶・ココア・ほうじ茶・黒ゴマ・マンゴー）
items.romaji: Toufu tiramisu (matcha / kokoa / houjicha / kurogoma / mangoo)
items.en: Tofu tiramisu — matcha / cocoa / hojicha / black sesame / mango
items.note: Flavor variants of the tofu tiramisu. Same wheat/dairy concern as the base — GF not confirmed; treat as wheat.
Agar (kanten) jelly-style tofu dessert; the jelly base is plausibly GF, but confirm no wheat-containing toppings, crumble, or sauce. Confirm no dairy/egg for vegan.
Strawberry milk drink; contains dairy (not vegan). Liquid likely GF, but confirm no cookie/wafer/biscuit garnish.

nara_authentic_table_tavern.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. À-la-carte prices are largely unpublished (own menu is an image only). Course prices from TableCheck; verify at booking.

items.note: Good GF candidate. Ask for it plain with salt only (塩焼き), no flour dusting, and any sauce served on the side or omitted.
Served simply blanched with salt and olive oil per two sources — naturally GF and vegan. Confirm no butter or sauce is added.

items.ja: 大和ポークの香草巻きロースト炭火炙り
items.romaji: Yamato pooku no kousou-maki roosuto sumibi-aburi
items.note: The herb wrap/coating and finishing jus (often flour-thickened) are the celiac risk. Ask for no flour dusting and sauce on the side; charcoal-grilled meat with salt is the safe request.

items.ja: 大和ポークの自家製ハム
items.note: House-cured ham is often GF, but cures/binders/accompanying sauce can contain wheat/soy. Ask that the cure and sauce are wheat-free; request no bread/crostini on the plate.
Soup curry is a thin spiced broth (not a flour roux), but the spice base or stock can still contain wheat. Ask 'スープのベースやスパイスに小麦は入っていますか' (any wheat in the base/spices?).

Includes soup, appetizer assortment, a pasta course (wheat), a main, and dessert. Give advance celiac notice; ask to omit/replace the pasta and any bread; take the charcoal-grilled main plain with salt.

Standard course includes bread and a choice of pasta — both wheat. Ask to skip bread and swap the pasta; choose a grilled main with sauce omitted/on side. Upcharge mains include lamb chops, duck confit, beef fillet.

nara_oniwa_shaveice_miracolo_2.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Open Wed/Thu only; rotating seasonal menu of ~4-6 flavors that changes daily, so a specific item may not be available on a given visit.

items.note: Nara 古都華 (Koto-ka) strawberry sauce with crème d'ange (egg + dairy, not vegan). Naturally GF but confirm the housemade strawberry syrup has no wheat/barley thickener. Spring-only.
Fresh pineapple + coconut syrup, topped with salted konpeito (sugar candy). Likely GF and possibly vegan — confirm the coconut syrup has no dairy and no wheat. Best vegan candidate.
Housemade spiced ginger syrup + ginger compote + crème d'ange (egg + dairy, not vegan). Ginger/spices usually GF but confirm the spice blend and housemade syrup have no wheat.

items.en: Kotoka Strawberry Milk Tea shaved ice
items.note: 古都華 Nara strawberry sauce + milk tea (dairy, not vegan). Tea/strawberry/milk have no wheat, but confirm the housemade strawberry syrup. Price from a single source.
Plain milk/condensed-milk white ice (dairy, not vegan). Matcha powder usually GF but confirm; confirm condensed milk/syrup have no wheat. Price from a single source.

nara_le_un_nipponia_hotel_nar.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Reservation-only counter kitchen; menu is seasonal. Give advance celiac notice at booking. Prices vary across sources.

items.ja: ランチコース A（Déjeuner A）
items.en: Lunch Course A (5 dishes, choice of main)
items.note: Fine-dining French course. Notify celiac needs well in advance. Kitchen can often adapt: request no bread and no flour-thickened sauces; Yamato pork/vegetables can be grilled/roasted plainly with salt.

items.ja: ランチコース B（Déjeuner B）
items.en: Lunch Course B (6 dishes, fish + meat double main)
items.note: Advance notice recommended. Ask to omit bread and flour-thickened/roux sauces; Yamato beef/pork can be prepared plainly.

Counter-style tasting dinner; reservation-required. Notify of celiac needs when booking. Showcases Yamato beef, pork, chicken and vegetables — proteins can be grilled/roasted with salt. Request no bread, no flour-thickened sauces.

items.ja: 大和ポークのロースト
items.en: Roast Yamato pork (red wine sauce, persimmon, walnuts, vegetables)
items.note: Recurring main. Pork itself is naturally GF; red wine sauce may be flour-thickened — ask for it plain or omitted. Good celiac-adaptable option with advance notice.

nara_omusubi_dokoro_wagaya.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Cash only, single-staff shop; takeout by advance Instagram reservation — worth messaging ahead about GF needs. Rice is Hokkaido Yumepirika (naturally GF).

items.note: The celiac-safe pick: just rice + salt, no filling. Confirm hands/utensils aren't cross-contaminated with soy-sauce fillings.
Umeboshi is GF; takuan (pickled daikon) is usually GF but seasoning/coloring varies. Ask about the takuan seasoning. Second-safest option after 塩.
Grilled salted salmon is usually GF, but confirm it wasn't marinated in a soy sauce/sake blend.
Miso can be barley-based (mugi miso) = gluten; ask whether it's rice miso (kome miso). Also confirm no dashi for vegan.
Contains pork. Miso may be barley-based; ask if rice miso.
Japanese mayo is typically GF, but canned tuna can contain wheat-derived broth/seasoning. Contains egg + fish.
Konbu tsukudani is simmered in soy sauce = wheat, unless tamari (unlikely). Treat as containing gluten.
Mentaiko curing liquid commonly contains soy sauce/wheat. Treat as containing gluten.
Okaka is normally dressed with soy sauce = wheat. Contains fish. Assume gluten unless they confirm tamari.
Soboro is simmered in soy sauce/mirin = wheat. Contains chicken. Assume gluten.

items.en: Omusubi set (2 choice omusubi + miso soup + small side + hojicha)
items.note: GF only if both omusubi are safe fillings (e.g. 塩). Miso soup uses bonito dashi (not vegan) and miso may contain barley; the rotating side is unknown — ask.

items.en: One-soup-three-sides set (2 omusubi + miso soup + 3 sides + hojicha)
items.note: Same cautions as omusubi set, plus 3 rotating sides (seen: tamagoyaki with dashi, nasu nibitashi, wiener stir-fry) that likely contain soy/wheat. Ask per side.

nara_washokoro_yoshino.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Traditional kaiseki: wheat-containing soy sauce, dashi, miso and tempura batter are pervasive. Nothing is reliably GF except plain rice. Advance celiac notice (事前連絡) and bringing certified tamari are essential.

items.en: Yoshino Lunch Set (sashimi, tempura, simmered dish, rice, miso soup, pickles, dessert)
items.note: Flagship set. Not safe as served: tempura batter is wheat, soy/dashi/miso used throughout. Give advance notice; bring tamari; ask to omit tempura and confirm dashi/miso.
Fish itself is naturally GF; the soy sauce served contains wheat. Bring your own tamari.

items.ja: 御飯（奈良県産コシヒカリ）
items.note: Plain white rice is GF, BUT it is served topped with chirimen-sansho (tiny soy-simmered fish) — ask for plain rice without the topping to keep it GF.
NOT gluten-free — tempura batter is wheat flour; tentsuyu contains soy/wheat. Ask to omit or substitute this course.
Simmered in soy sauce + dashi — both wheat/gluten risks. Treat as off-limits unless kitchen confirms wheat-free seasoning.

items.ja: 大和三尺きゅうりの奈良漬
items.note: Narazuke is pickled in sake lees (usually GF) but some producers add mirin/soy. Confirm before eating.
Assume pervasive soy/dashi/wheat. Advance notice + bring tamari; ask for salt-grilled (塩焼き) rather than tare, and confirm dashi.
Signature dinner course; selectable main. Sashimi/grilled/simmered courses — soy/dashi/wheat pervasive. Advance celiac notice essential; bring tamari; ask for salt-grilled.
Top-tier multi-course kaiseki. Highest number of soy/dashi/wheat touchpoints. Advance celiac notice required; bring tamari; request salt-grilled fish.

items.en: Dinner Course (appetizer, sushi, main dish, dessert, Japanese tea)
items.note: Fully dedicated gluten-free, vegan and nut-free kitchen — no wheat, soba or peanuts used anywhere; umami built from kombu/shiitake/vegetable stock. Served 15:00–20:00 (LO 19:00). Prices not published online.

items.en: Dessert Set (muffin, ice cream, seasonal fruit + drink)
items.note: All sweets made without wheat; dedicated GF kitchen.

items.ja: テイスティング日本酒セット
items.en: Tasting Sake Set (3 Nara sakes, 60ml each)
items.note: Junmai sake is rice-based and wheat-free. Confirm no wheat-containing seasonings.

items.en: Seasonal Enzyme Juice (spicy lemon / ume plum / yuzu)
items.note: House fermented-fruit juice; dedicated GF kitchen.
Naturally gluten-free.

items.ja: ルバーブタルト（グルテンフリー＆ヴィーガン）
items.note: 100% gluten-free, wheat-free vegan kitchen. Listed price is a whole tart (online/frozen retail); in-cafe slice pricing may differ.
Whole-tart retail price; may be seasonally sold out.
Whole-tart retail price.

items.ja: ヴィーガン抹茶バスクチーズケーキ
items.note: Whole-cake retail price; may be sold out.

items.ja: ヴィーガンバスクチーズケーキ
items.note: Whole-cake retail price; may be sold out.

items.ja: グルテンフリー美活御膳
items.en: Gluten-Free Bikatsu Gozen (signature healthy set, monthly-changing; incl. tea/coffee)
items.note: Marketed gluten-free set, limited to 15/day. WARNING: shared kitchen — beer/wine/sake and wheat items are also served, and an Oct 2025 celiac glutening was traced to a sauce here. Tell staff you have celiac disease and confirm each component.
Bun gluten-status not confirmed; shared kitchen. Do not assume GF without checking.

items.ja: ビッグマウンテンベジバーガー
items.note: Plant-based, but bun gluten-status not confirmed; shared kitchen.

items.ja: 自家製ジェノベーゼのモッツァレラピザ
items.note: Crust gluten-status not confirmed; shared kitchen.
Naturally plant-based but dressing may contain soy sauce (wheat); confirm.
Mochiko is rice flour, but the marinade/sauce may contain wheat — a documented celiac glutening at this cafe was traced to a chicken sauce. Do NOT assume gluten-free.
Contents vary; shared kitchen — confirm gluten with staff.
GF/vegan baked good sourced from a local dedicated GF maker (Somi). Served in a non-dedicated cafe that also makes wheat hot sandwiches — confirm current batch and cross-contamination.
GF/vegan baked good; shared cafe environment — confirm.
Naturally gluten-free.
Dairy milk (plant milk may be available); coffee itself is gluten-free.
Spiced tea latte; naturally gluten-free.
Granola often contains oats/barley — gluten-status not confirmed. Confirm before ordering.
Made on wheat bread — NOT gluten-free.

items.ja: 季節の果物のジュース
items.note: 100% fresh fruit, naturally gluten-free. No celiac/allergen program — confirm no shared equipment concerns.
Fresh fruit juice; naturally gluten-free.
Freshly squeezed; naturally gluten-free.
Freshly squeezed; naturally gluten-free.
Banana + dairy milk; naturally gluten-free but not vegan.
Shaved ice with seasonal fruit and milk; naturally gluten-free (not vegan). Confirm syrups.

items.en: Strawberry Milk Shaved Ice (with milk pudding)
items.note: Frozen strawberry slices + milk pudding on shaved ice; naturally gluten-free (not vegan). Confirm pudding ingredients.
Just roasted sweet potato — naturally gluten-free and vegan.
Signature slow-aged twice-roasted honey sweet potato; plain roasted sweet potato is naturally gluten-free. Cafe also handles wheat items — confirm no cross-contact.
Plain roasted sweet potato — naturally gluten-free.
Plain roasted sweet potato — naturally gluten-free.
Plain roasted sweet potato — naturally gluten-free.

items.en: Candied (daigaku) sweet potato in sweet-potato syrup
items.note: Deep-fried in shared oil; coating and fryer not confirmed gluten-free.

items.ja: 安納芋のチーズケーキセット
items.note: Cheesecake — base/crumb typically contains wheat.

items.ja: 紫芋のチーズケーキセット

items.ja: スイートポテトパイセット
items.note: Pastry — contains wheat.

items.ja: 蜜芋のパイ包みセット
items.en: Honey sweet potato wrapped in pie set
items.note: Pastry — contains wheat.

items.ja: 丸ごとスイートポテト
items.note: May contain butter/flour — not confirmed gluten-free.
Dairy soft serve; cone contains wheat.
Syrup/topping not confirmed gluten-free.

items.ja: お芋のデザートプレート
items.note: Assorted plate — includes wheat-based items.

items.ja: お芋屋さんのコロッケ
items.note: Breaded and fried — contains wheat.

Fish paste typically bound with wheat starch.

items.en: Tofu shop's thick fried tofu, grilled
items.note: Fried tofu itself is GF; served with soy sauce/ginger topping — ask to skip the soy. Often has bonito.
Often carries a mirin/soy glaze and is served with mayonnaise — ask.
Grilled mushroom is naturally GF — ask for salt/sudachi and no soy tare or bonito.

items.ja: 焼き万願寺とうがらし
items.note: Grilled pepper is naturally GF — usually topped with soy and bonito, so ask for salt only.

items.ja: 海老 姿焼き 一尾
items.note: Salt-grilled prawn — gluten-free as served.
Salt-grilled — gluten-free as served.
Salt-grilled — gluten-free as served.

items.en: Chicken skin with meat in ponzu
items.note: Ponzu contains soy sauce (wheat) — ask for salt and citrus.
Takowasa seasoning is soy-based (wheat).
Miso may be barley-based and the spicy sauce may contain wheat.
Some pickles are soy-brined (wheat) — ask which are safe.
Boiled and salted — gluten-free.

items.en: Spicy fermented cod guts smothered in scallion
items.note: Gochujang/chanja seasoning typically contains wheat.
Tofu is GF but may be marinated in soy before smoking — ask.
Nara-zuke is pickled in rice sake-lees (no wheat) but confirm; contains cheese.
Traditional Nara-zuke is pickled in rice sake-lees (contains alcohol, no wheat) — confirm no soy is used.
Ask for dressing on the side; standard dressing is soy-based.

items.note: Croutons and Caesar dressing contain wheat.

items.en: Prosciutto and soft-boiled egg salad (regular / half)
items.note: Ask for dressing on the side.
Rice cooked in soy-based seasoning (wheat).
Rice cooked in soy-based seasoning (wheat).

items.ja: うなぎのひつまぶし風釜めし
items.note: Eel kabayaki glaze plus soy seasoning — wheat.

items.note: Seasoning likely contains soy/wheat.
Rice cooked in soy-based dashi (wheat).

items.ja: いちごのアイスケーキ

items.en: Hana-musubi set — two rice balls (Yamato Kogen Koshihikari rice), grilled fish, a side, and miso soup
items.note: Rice balls and salt-grilled fish are GF; the miso soup may use barley miso or a soy/dashi base — ask. Choose plain fillings (see below).
Plain salted rice — gluten-free.
Umeboshi and rice — gluten-free.
Salt-salmon and rice — gluten-free.
Mentaiko is often seasoned with soy/additives — ask.
